reasoning: Elon Musk left his role as a Special Government Employee (SGE) in the Trump administration in late May 2025, specifically noting the imminent expiration of his 130-day legal limit for SGEs. Following his exit, Musk had a significant public falling-out with Donald Trump, culminating in Musk vowing to start a third political party and Trump calling Musk 'off the rails' by July 2025. By late October 2025, despite Trump rhetorically taking credit for putting Musk 'in charge' of DOGE in the past, the administration officially stated Musk was not an administrator. Because the SGE rules limit service to 130 days within a 365-day period, Musk legally cannot be reappointed as an SGE in 2025. Returning in a full-time capacity would require divesting his multibillion-dollar companies, which is highly implausible. Combined with their public political feud, a reappointment before the end of 2025 is exceptionally unlikely.
